growth_records.strain: freshwater lake isolate (MN/VA, USA); this study
growth_records.medium: Defined P-rich mineral medium (Tanner, 2007), glucose C source, P-gradient (C:P_R 10-5000)
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% μmax for each isolate and operated and sampled as described above.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Actinobacteria). Steady-state chemostat: μ = D = 0.06 h−1 (Table 1). μmax = 0.06/0.25 = 0.24 h−1. td = ln2/0.06 = 11.55 h. Glucose carbon source; no uptake/secretion rates reported. Biomass C:P and homeostasis from Table 1. Temp 25 C; pH not reported.
